assertions.title: Create User Form Validates and Submits

steps.observed: Users list page rendered with DataTable showing 7 users

steps.expected: Create User dialog opens with form fields
steps.observed: Dialog opened with Email, Display Name, Role checkboxes (ROLE_ADMIN, ROLE_ACCOUNTING, ROLE_FACTORY, ROLE_SALES, ROLE_DEALER), Company assignment checkbox (Mock Training Co), Cancel and Create User buttons

steps.action: Click Create User with empty fields
steps.expected: Validation errors shown for required fields
steps.observed:
Validation errors displayed: 'Email is required' (text-[var(--color-error)]) and 'Display name is required' — both rendered as

elements with error styling

steps.action: Fill form with valid data and click Create User
steps.observed: POST /admin/users returned 500 (Internal Server Error). Toast displayed: 'Failed to create user — Request failed with status code 500'. No console JS errors — the error is purely a backend issue.

evidence.consoleErrors: none (500 is a backend error, not a frontend JS error)

assertions.issues: Frontend form and validation work correctly. Backend POST /admin/users still returns 500 — backend bug persists from Round 1. Form renders correctly with all required fields, validation fires on empty submit with proper error messages, but successful creation is blocked by backend.

steps.observed: Users list rendered with 7 user rows

steps.action: Click row action dropdown (three dots) on Validation Accounting user
steps.expected: Dropdown menu opens with Suspend option
steps.observed: Dropdown opened successfully showing: View, Edit, Suspend, Force reset password, Delete. Event propagation fix confirmed working — dropdown no longer triggers row onClick.
Confirmation dialog appeared with 'Cancel' and 'Suspend' buttons

steps.expected: API call PATCH /admin/users/{id}/suspend succeeds, user status changes to Suspended
steps.observed: PATCH /api/v1/admin/users/2/suspend was sent (appeared twice in network log, suggesting retry). Toast showed 'Action failed' initially, but after page reload the user status correctly shows 'Suspended'. Verified via direct API call that backend returned 204 (success). The initial 'Action failed' toast appears to be a timing/race condition — the API call actually succeeded.

steps.action: Reload page and verify user status
steps.expected: Validation Accounting user shows 'Suspended' status badge
steps.observed: After reload, user row 2 (Validation Accounting) shows status 'Suspended' in the table. All other users remain 'Active'.

steps.action: Click row action dropdown on Validation Sales user (user 3)
steps.expected: Dropdown menu opens with Force reset password option
steps.observed: Dropdown opened showing: View, Edit, Suspend, Force reset password, Delete. Event propagation fix confirmed working.
Confirmation dialog appeared with 'Cancel' and 'Send reset link' buttons

POST /api/v1/admin/users/3/force-reset-password sent. Users list refreshed after the call. Verified via direct API call that endpoint returns 200 with {success: true, message: 'Password reset link sent'}. A 'Dismiss' toast briefly appeared (success notification).

evidence.network: POST /api/v1/admin/users/3/force-reset-password -> 200 {success: true, message: 'Password reset link sent'}

assertions.title: Disable MFA for User

steps.action: Check all users for MFA enabled status
steps.expected: At least one user has MFA enabled to test disable action
steps.observed: Checked all users via DOM evaluation. No users have mfaEnabled=true. User list: validation.admin@example.com (mfaEnabled: false), validation.accounting@example.com (false), validation.sales@example.com (false), validation.factory@example.com (false), validation.dealer@example.com (false), validation.superadmin@example.com (false), utest_validate_g2@test.com (false). Additionally, no 'Disable MFA' button appears in any user's dropdown menu — the UI correctly hides the action when no users have MFA enabled.
Verified via direct API call to backend: all users have mfaEnabled=false or the field is not populated in the response.

assertions.issues: Blocked: No test users have MFA enabled in the backend seed data. Same blocker as Round 1. The frontend code exists to show 'Disable MFA' in the dropdown (conditionally rendered based on user.mfaEnabled), but cannot be tested without MFA-enabled users.

frictions.description: VAL-ADMIN-006 showed 'Action failed' toast despite the API call succeeding (204 response). This appears to be a race condition where the toast is shown before the retry succeeds. After page reload, the user status correctly shows 'Suspended'.
frictions.resolution: Verified backend state via direct API call — suspend was successful. Toast message is misleading but the action works correctly.

blockers.description: Backend POST /admin/users returns 500 — user creation is not possible. This is a backend-side bug that has persisted since Round 1.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified via direct curl to backend (localhost:8081) — still returns 500. Frontend form and validation work correctly.

blockers.description: No test users have MFA enabled in backend seed data — cannot test 'Disable MFA' action. Requires backend seed data update to enable MFA on at least one test user.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked all users via API — all have mfaEnabled: false.

summary: Tested 4 assertions in Round 2: 2 passed (VAL-ADMIN-006, VAL-ADMIN-007 — event propagation fix confirmed working for both suspend and force password reset actions), 2 blocked (VAL-ADMIN-004 — backend 500 on user creation persists, VAL-ADMIN-008 — no MFA-enabled users in test data). The DropdownMenu event propagation fix from commit 7f93818 is confirmed resolved.
